text.title: Backup Strategy Basics (3-2-1-1-0 + RPO/RTO) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Backup Strategy Basics (3-2-1-1-0 + RPO/RTO)` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Strategy MUST satisfy 3-2-1 as a minimum (3 copies, 2 media, 1 offsite). Production-critical data MUST satisfy 3-2-1-1-0 (add immutable / air-gapped + verified).
rule.rationale: 3-2-1 is the testable minimum; 1-0 adds ransomware survivability + verification.

rule.statement: Each data tier MUST publish RPO + RTO numbers (e.g. tier-A RPO ≤ 1h, RTO ≤ 4h). `as-soon-as-possible` is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Vague RPO/RTO makes restore strategy untestable.

rule.statement: At least one backup copy MUST be immutable (Object Lock / WORM / air-gap) for production-critical data.
rule.rationale: Mutable backups can be deleted by the same incident that triggered the restore.

rule.statement: Restore drills MUST be run on a published cadence (≤ quarterly per critical tier). Drill outcomes feed back into RTO numbers.
rule.rationale: Untested backups are folklore; only a drilled restore proves RTO.

rule.statement: Strategy MUST list a named DR owner accountable for restore drills + retention reviews.
rule.rationale: Anonymous backup strategies decay; named owner forces cadence.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
